channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: McCall MacBain Scholarship 2027 in Canada: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarship Covering Tuition, Fees, Living Stipend, Mentorship, and Leadership Development
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Students and graduates from around the world now have the opportunity to pursue graduate studies in Canada through the highly competitive McCall MacBain Scholarship 2027 at McGill University. This fully funded scholarship is designed to support future leaders who demonstrate exceptional character, community engagement, leadership potential, entrepreneurial spirit, and academic excellence. The McCall MacBain Scholarship […]

The post McCall MacBain Scholarship 2027 in Canada: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarship Covering Tuition, Fees, Living Stipend, Mentorship, and Leadership Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and graduates from around the world now have the opportunity to pursue graduate studies in Canada through the highly competitive McCall MacBain Scholarship 2027 at McGill University. This fully funded scholarship is designed to support future leaders who demonstrate exceptional character, community engagement, leadership potential, entrepreneurial spirit, and academic excellence.

The McCall MacBain Scholarship goes far beyond financial support. In addition to covering tuition and fees, the program provides mentorship, leadership development opportunities, interdisciplinary learning experiences, and access to a global network of scholars and changemakers.

For students who aspire to make a meaningful impact in their communities and professions, this scholarship offers a unique platform to develop the skills, knowledge, and connections needed to create positive change on a global scale.

What is the McCall MacBain Scholarship?

The McCall MacBain Scholarship is a comprehensive graduate scholarship program hosted at McGill University in Montreal, Canada. The scholarship was created to empower talented individuals who have demonstrated a commitment to leadership, service, and community impact.

The program aims to bring together outstanding students from diverse academic disciplines and cultural backgrounds while fostering collaboration, innovation, and leadership development.

Selected scholars become part of a vibrant learning community where they receive mentorship from experienced leaders, participate in specialized leadership programming, and engage in interdisciplinary learning opportunities throughout their studies.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive funding package that significantly reduces the financial burden of graduate education.

The scholarship benefits include:

  • Full tuition coverage for the normal duration of an eligible master’s or professional program.
  • Coverage of mandatory fees associated with the program.
  • Monthly living stipend of CAD $2,300 during academic terms.
  • One-time relocation grant to assist with moving to Montreal.
  • Access to mentorship opportunities from experienced leaders.
  • Participation in interdisciplinary learning experiences.
  • Membership in a global community of scholars and changemakers.
  • Leadership development programming designed to strengthen personal and professional growth.

What Makes the McCall MacBain Scholarship Unique?

Unlike many graduate scholarships that focus solely on academic achievement, the McCall MacBain Scholarship evaluates applicants holistically.

The selection process focuses on:

1. Exceptional Character

Applicants are expected to demonstrate integrity, empathy, honesty, perseverance, and a commitment to lifelong learning.

2. Community Engagement

The scholarship values individuals who actively contribute to improving their communities and who have dedicated time and effort to helping others.

3. Leadership Potential

Candidates should have experience working with and leading others toward common goals while creating meaningful impact.

4. Entrepreneurial Spirit

Applicants who identify challenges, develop innovative solutions, and inspire others to take action are highly valued.

5. Academic Strength and Intellectual Curiosity

Strong academic performance and a genuine curiosity about people, ideas, and global issues are important components of the selection process.

Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for the McCall MacBain Scholarship 2027, applicants must satisfy at least one of the following requirements:

  1. Be currently completing a first bachelor’s degree and expected to graduate by August 2027.
  2. Have earned a first bachelor’s degree within the last five years (January 2021 or later).
  3. Have earned a first bachelor’s degree more than five years ago and have been 30 years old or younger as of January 1, 2026.

Applicants must also meet the admission requirements of their intended eligible graduate program at McGill University, including any language requirements.

Application Components

The McCall MacBain Scholarship application is designed to help the selection committee understand each applicant’s background, achievements, aspirations, and potential.

Applicants will be asked to submit:

Personal Information

This section includes information about:

  • Family background.
  • Educational history.
  • Languages spoken.
  • Scholarships, distinctions, and awards received.

Academic and Career Interests

Applicants explain their academic goals, career aspirations, and future plans.

Activities and Community Involvement

Candidates provide details of leadership experiences, extracurricular activities, community engagement, and employment history.

References

Applicants must submit references who can provide insight into their abilities, character, and achievements.

Endorsement

Some candidates may require endorsement through participating institutions or universities.

Short Answers and Essays

Applicants respond to essay questions that help the committee understand their experiences, values, and leadership potential.

Resume

A current resume outlining educational background, leadership experiences, achievements, and work history.

Academic Transcripts

Official academic records must be provided as part of the application.

Waiver and Confirmation

Applicants confirm that all submitted materials represent their own work and experiences.

Application Tips from the Scholarship Program

The scholarship team encourages applicants to focus on authenticity throughout the application process.

Some important recommendations include:

  • Write clearly and concisely.
  • Use simple and direct language.
  • Avoid unnecessary complexity and jargon.
  • Focus on telling your personal story.
  • Reflect honestly on your strengths and areas for growth.
  • Demonstrate how your experiences have shaped your goals.
  • Highlight meaningful contributions to your community.
  • Showcase leadership experiences and lessons learned.

Applicants are reminded that the application should be entirely their own work and should reflect their genuine voice and experiences.

Selection and Interview Process

The McCall MacBain Scholarship selection process consists of multiple stages.

Stage 1: Application Review

Applications are reviewed based on leadership potential, community engagement, character, entrepreneurial spirit, and academic achievement.

Stage 2: Regional Interviews

More than 250 semi-finalists are expected to be invited to regional interviews conducted between October and November 2026.

International candidates participate in online regional interviews based on their geographic region.

Stage 3: Final Interviews

Approximately 85 finalists will be invited to attend final interviews in Montreal in March 2027.

Travel expenses for finalists are covered by the scholarship program. Finalists may receive either the full scholarship or a finalist award.

Stage 4: Scholarship Award

Up to 30 scholars will be selected to begin their studies at McGill University in September 2027. The cohort includes both Canadian and international scholars.

Application Deadline

The application deadlines for the 2027 McCall MacBain Scholarship cohort are:

International Applicants

August 19, 2026 (4:00 PM Eastern Time)

Applicants from Canada and the United States

September 23, 2026 (4:00 PM Eastern Time)

Why You Should Apply

The McCall MacBain Scholarship is one of the most comprehensive graduate funding opportunities available in Canada. Beyond financial support, it provides access to mentorship, leadership training, interdisciplinary learning, and a diverse international community committed to creating positive change.

If you are passionate about leadership, service, innovation, and making a lasting impact in your field and community, this scholarship could be a transformative opportunity for your academic and professional journey.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should prepare their application materials early and carefully review the scholarship requirements and eligible graduate programs at McGill University.

Click here for more and to apply

Click here for more global Scholarship opportunities

The post McCall MacBain Scholarship 2027 in Canada: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarship Covering Tuition, Fees, Living Stipend, Mentorship, and Leadership Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Disability Rights Fund 2026 Grant Round for Sub-Saharan Africa: Funding Opportunities for Organizations of Persons with Disabilities
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Organizations of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs) across Sub-Saharan Africa often face significant challenges in securing sustainable funding for their advocacy, policy work, and community-driven initiatives. Many OPDs rely heavily on volunteer commitment and grassroots support to advance disability rights, making access to dedicated funding both essential and transformative. One of the most significant opportunities for […]

The post Disability Rights Fund 2026 Grant Round for Sub-Saharan Africa: Funding Opportunities for Organizations of Persons with Disabilities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs) across Sub-Saharan Africa often face significant challenges in securing sustainable funding for their advocacy, policy work, and community-driven initiatives. Many OPDs rely heavily on volunteer commitment and grassroots support to advance disability rights, making access to dedicated funding both essential and transformative. One of the most significant opportunities for disability-led organizations is the upcoming 2026 grant round from the Disability Rights Fund (DRF).

This grant opportunity provides critical financial support to organizations led by persons with disabilities, enabling them to advocate for rights, influence policies, and strengthen disability inclusion efforts across the continent. As the 2026 grant cycle approaches, eligible organizations should begin preparing early to maximize their chances of securing funding.

What Is the Disability Rights Fund?

Founded in 2008, the Disability Rights Fund is a pooled grantmaking mechanism dedicated exclusively to supporting organizations led by persons with disabilities. The fund works alongside its sister organization, the Disability Rights Advocacy Fund (DRAF), which specifically supports advocacy initiatives related to legislative change and policy reform.

Together, DRF and DRAF have provided funding to hundreds of OPDs across several regions, including:

  • Sub-Saharan Africa

  • South Asia

  • Southeast Asia

  • Haiti

  • Pacific Island Countries

A defining feature of the Disability Rights Fund is its governance structure. Persons with disabilities are not merely beneficiaries of the grants—they are actively involved in decision-making. Members of the disability community participate in the Grantmaking Committee, ensuring that funding decisions reflect the priorities and lived experiences of people with disabilities.

This governance model reflects the principle widely embraced within the disability rights movement: “Nothing about us without us.”

Transparency is another key pillar of the DRF’s operations. Since its creation, the organization has maintained a public directory tracking every grant awarded from 2008 through 2024, reinforcing accountability to the disability rights movement and its partners.

Overview of the 2026 Grant Round

The Disability Rights Fund continues to expand its support for disability-led organizations. A major development in recent grant cycles has been the expansion of eligibility to all countries across Sub-Saharan Africa, moving beyond earlier geographic limitations.

The 2026 grant round is expected to follow a similar structure to previous cycles and will likely include three main funding streams designed to support different types of organizations and coalitions.

Small Grants

Small grants are designed to support local or sub-national organizations of persons with disabilities that are working directly within communities.

These grants can provide up to USD 60,000 over two years, helping smaller OPDs strengthen advocacy campaigns, implement programs, and expand their organizational capacity.

Mid-Level Coalition Grants

Mid-level coalition grants support collaborative initiatives involving three or more organizations. These grants recognize that collective action can amplify the impact of disability advocacy efforts.

Coalitions can receive between USD 30,000 and USD 50,000 per year to support coordinated advocacy campaigns, policy engagement, and strategic initiatives.

National Coalition Grants

National coalition grants are designed for larger national-level partnerships advocating for systemic change.

These grants provide USD 40,000 to USD 60,000 per year, with a total potential funding amount of up to USD 120,000 over two years.

By supporting coalitions, DRF encourages collaborative efforts that strengthen the disability rights movement at national and regional levels.

Application Process: Letter of Interest (LOI)

Applications for the Disability Rights Fund begin with a Letter of Interest (LOI) submission. This initial stage allows organizations to present their proposed project and organizational profile before being invited to submit a full proposal.

LOI forms are made accessible in multiple languages, including:

  • English

  • French

  • Portuguese

  • Indonesian

The availability of forms in French is particularly important for Francophone African organizations, ensuring that language barriers do not prevent participation in the grant process.

Eligibility Requirements

The Disability Rights Fund has clear eligibility criteria to ensure that funding directly benefits disability-led organizations.

To qualify, an applicant organization must:

  • Be headquartered and primarily operating in a Sub-Saharan African country targeted by DRF

  • Be a registered organization of persons with disabilities (OPD)

  • Alternatively operate under a fiscal sponsor that is a registered OPD

  • Propose a project aligned with the United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD)

DRF emphasizes projects that promote implementation and monitoring of the CRPD at local, national, or regional levels.

Activities That Are Not Funded

It is important for applicants to understand what types of activities are not eligible for DRF funding.

The fund does not support:

  • Individual applicants

  • Construction projects or building accessibility upgrades

  • Direct service provision programs

  • Income generation initiatives

  • Legislative lobbying activities (these fall under DRAF funding streams)

Organizations applying for coalition grants must also ensure that the lead organization is an OPD, although coalition partners may include other civil society organizations working on human rights or social justice.

Timeline for the 2026 Grant Cycle

Organizations planning to apply should carefully note the key dates for the 2026 grant round.

  • Portal opens for LOI submissions: May 30, 2026

  • Deadline for Letters of Interest: June 21, 2026 at 23:59 ET

  • Invitations for successful LOIs: July 28, 2026

  • Deadline for full applications (invited organizations only): August 23, 2026

  • Final grant decisions announced: December 2026

  • Successful projects begin: January 1, 2027

Preparing well in advance is strongly recommended, as previous grant rounds have received extremely high numbers of applications.

Multi-Year Funding: A Major Advantage for OPDs

One of the most impactful changes introduced by the Disability Rights Fund in recent years is the option for multi-year funding.

Beginning with the 2023 grant cycle, eligible returning grantees can apply for two years of funding through a single proposal process.

This change addresses a major challenge faced by many disability organizations: the administrative burden of annual grant cycles. By providing multi-year funding, DRF allows organizations to focus more time and energy on advocacy and program implementation rather than constant fundraising.

Multi-year funding also enables organizations to:

  • Plan longer-term advocacy campaigns

  • Hire staff with greater job security

  • Strengthen internal governance and operational capacity

Preparing a Strong Letter of Interest

Given the competitive nature of the DRF grants, organizations should begin preparing their applications early.

Strong LOIs typically include the following elements:

Clear alignment with the CRPD

Applicants should identify the specific articles of the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities that their project supports.

Demonstration of disability-led leadership

DRF prioritizes organizations where persons with disabilities occupy leadership positions and decision-making roles.

Strategic advocacy focus

Projects linked to global disability advocacy initiatives—such as commitments made through the Global Disability Summit—can strengthen an application.

Organizational documentation

Applicants should prepare essential documents in advance, including:

  • Organizational background information

  • Financial statements

  • Governance documentation

Organizations that are not formally registered should also identify a fiscal sponsor that is a registered OPD.

Additional Funding Opportunities for Disability Organizations

While preparing for the DRF grant round, organizations may also explore other funding opportunities that support disability inclusion.

Several international foundations and development institutions provide funding for disability-related initiatives, including:

  • Open Society Foundations

  • Ford Foundation

  • CBM Global

  • African Development Bank

Platforms such as ReliefWeb and Devex Opportunities also provide listings of disability-related grants and development funding opportunities.

Final Thoughts

The Disability Rights Fund represents one of the most important funding mechanisms for organizations led by persons with disabilities in Sub-Saharan Africa. By supporting advocacy aligned with the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities, the fund strengthens the global disability rights movement and empowers local organizations to influence policies and advance inclusion.

As the 2026 grant round approaches, organizations should begin preparing their documentation, strengthening partnerships with other OPDs, and aligning their advocacy strategies with international disability rights frameworks.

The opportunity is designed specifically for disability-led organizations working to advance equality, inclusion, and human rights across Africa.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Disability Rights Fund 2026 Grant Round for Sub-Saharan Africa: Funding Opportunities for Organizations of Persons with Disabilities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Pulitzer Center Artificial Intelligence Accountability Fellowships 2026–2027: Up to $25,000 Funding for Journalists Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Pulitzer Center has officially opened applications for its prestigious Artificial Intelligence Accountability Fellowships 2026–2027, offering journalists from around the world an opportunity to investigate some of the most pressing issues surrounding artificial intelligence and automated systems. As artificial intelligence continues to transform governments, businesses, workplaces, public services, and everyday life, the need for independent, […]

The post Pulitzer Center Artificial Intelligence Accountability Fellowships 2026–2027: Up to $25,000 Funding for Journalists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pulitzer Center has officially opened applications for its prestigious Artificial Intelligence Accountability Fellowships 2026–2027, offering journalists from around the world an opportunity to investigate some of the most pressing issues surrounding artificial intelligence and automated systems.

As artificial intelligence continues to transform governments, businesses, workplaces, public services, and everyday life, the need for independent, evidence-based journalism has become more important than ever. The fellowship seeks to support journalists who are committed to uncovering the real-world impacts of AI technologies, exploring both their opportunities and risks, and holding powerful institutions accountable for how these technologies are designed, funded, and deployed.

Selected fellows will receive up to $25,000 in funding, along with mentorship, specialized training, and access to a global network of journalists and experts dedicated to AI accountability reporting.

About the Pulitzer Center Artificial Intelligence Accountability Fellowships

The Artificial Intelligence Accountability Fellowship was established to strengthen public-interest journalism focused on AI and algorithmic systems. Since its launch in 2022, the fellowship has supported dozens of journalists across five continents, helping them produce impactful investigations that have influenced public debate, informed policymaking, and strengthened accountability efforts worldwide.

The fellowship supports ambitious reporting projects that examine:

  • The social impact of artificial intelligence systems
  • Government use of AI technologies
  • Corporate influence in AI development and deployment
  • Labor conditions within the AI industry
  • Data collection and surveillance practices
  • Algorithmic decision-making systems
  • AI regulation and governance
  • Human rights implications of AI technologies
  • AI supply chains and infrastructure
  • Public accountability and transparency issues

The program is designed to help journalists produce rigorous investigations that reveal how AI systems affect communities and societies across the globe.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive a comprehensive package of support to strengthen their reporting projects.

Financial Support

Each fellow may receive up to:

  • $20,000 for reporting expenses
  • $5,000 for engagement and impact activities

The total funding available is up to $25,000 per fellow.

Eligible Use of Funds

Funding may be used for:

  • Investigative reporting expenses
  • Travel costs
  • Data analysis
  • Records requests
  • Research activities
  • Reporting stipends
  • Audience engagement initiatives
  • Community outreach activities
  • Impact-focused dissemination efforts

Mentorship and Training

Fellows will gain access to:

  • Experienced mentors from diverse professional backgrounds
  • Specialized AI reporting training
  • Editorial guidance
  • Peer-learning opportunities
  • Professional development resources
  • Collaborative learning environments

New Impact Planning Component

For the first time, the fellowship includes dedicated support for developing and implementing impact strategies for investigative projects.

This additional support may include:

  • Partnerships with content creators
  • Community engagement initiatives
  • Public education campaigns
  • Local information products
  • Stakeholder dialogues
  • Civil society collaborations
  • Outreach to policymakers and decision-makers

The goal is to ensure that investigations reach audiences who can drive meaningful conversations and change.

Examples of Previous Fellowship Investigations

Past fellows have produced influential reporting on topics such as:

  • Data labeling work connected to military applications
  • Technology companies’ strategies for building large-scale data centers
  • Algorithmic flaws affecting social welfare programs
  • Export of facial recognition technologies to countries with limited regulatory oversight
  • AI-driven systems impacting vulnerable communities

These investigations have generated significant impact, including:

  • Contributions to new legislation
  • Evidence supporting legal actions
  • Increased public awareness
  • Educational initiatives and student-led investigations
  • Greater scrutiny of AI systems and corporate practices

Fellowship Duration

The fellowship runs for 10 months, beginning in September 2026.

Throughout the program, fellows will work on their reporting projects while participating in training, mentorship, and collaborative activities.

Fellowship Requirements

Successful applicants must be prepared to actively participate throughout the fellowship period.

Requirements include:

Mandatory Monthly Meetings

Fellows must attend:

  • Monthly virtual meetings lasting approximately 1.5 to 2 hours

Community Participation

Participants will be expected to:

  • Contribute to at least one community call
  • Engage with fellow journalists
  • Participate in discussions on the fellowship platform
  • Collaborate with the global fellowship community

Training Sessions

Fellows are encouraged to attend:

  • Monthly virtual training sessions
  • Specialized workshops and learning opportunities

Knowledge Sharing

Participants are required to:

  • Share reporting methodologies
  • Document lessons learned
  • Contribute insights that can help other journalists pursuing similar investigations

This collaborative approach helps strengthen the broader field of AI accountability journalism and supports the development of best practices across newsrooms worldwide.

Why Artificial Intelligence Accountability Reporting Matters

Artificial intelligence technologies are increasingly shaping critical aspects of modern life, including:

  • Employment
  • Healthcare
  • Education
  • Public services
  • Law enforcement
  • Financial systems
  • Social welfare programs
  • Communication platforms

Despite their growing influence, many AI systems operate with limited transparency and oversight. Independent journalism plays a vital role in helping the public understand how these technologies function and how they affect individuals and communities.

The Pulitzer Center’s fellowship aims to strengthen reporting that investigates these complex systems while amplifying stories that might otherwise remain hidden from public view.

About the Artificial Intelligence Accountability Network

The fellowship operates under the Pulitzer Center’s Artificial Intelligence Accountability Network, launched in 2022 to expand and diversify the field of AI reporting in the public interest.

The network also supports several complementary initiatives, including:

Artificial Intelligence Reporting Grants

These grants support shorter-term reporting projects examining AI’s impact on communities around the world.

Artificial Intelligence Spotlight Series

A free online training program that equips journalists and editors with the knowledge and skills needed to cover AI effectively.

South to South Artificial Intelligence Accountability Collaborative Laboratory

A collaborative initiative connecting journalists, researchers, civil society organizations, and academic institutions across:

  • Africa
  • Latin America
  • South Asia
  • Southeast Asia

The program seeks to strengthen relationships between journalism and civil society engagement across the Global South.

Application Deadline

Application Deadline: July 12, 2026

Interested journalists are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute challenges.

How to Apply

Applicants should prepare a strong investigative reporting proposal that demonstrates:

  • Clear relevance to AI accountability
  • Potential public impact
  • Feasibility of execution
  • Strong reporting methodology
  • Commitment to public-interest journalism

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Pulitzer Center Artificial Intelligence Accountability Fellowships 2026–2027: Up to $25,000 Funding for Journalists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Food Programme Junior Professional Officer Opportunities 2026 for Canadian Nationals: Fully Funded International Humanitarian Careers in Senegal and Italy
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The World Food Programme (WFP), the world’s largest humanitarian organization and the recipient of the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize, is currently inviting applications from qualified Canadian nationals for two prestigious Junior Professional Officer (JPO) positions based in Dakar, Senegal, and Rome, Italy. These highly competitive international opportunities provide young professionals with the chance to build […]

The post World Food Programme Junior Professional Officer Opportunities 2026 for Canadian Nationals: Fully Funded International Humanitarian Careers in Senegal and Italy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Food Programme (WFP), the world’s largest humanitarian organization and the recipient of the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize, is currently inviting applications from qualified Canadian nationals for two prestigious Junior Professional Officer (JPO) positions based in Dakar, Senegal, and Rome, Italy.

These highly competitive international opportunities provide young professionals with the chance to build impactful careers within the United Nations system while contributing to global efforts aimed at ending hunger, improving food security, and supporting vulnerable populations affected by conflict, disasters, and climate change.

The available positions are:

  1. JPO Logistics Officer – Dakar, Senegal
  2. JPO Programme Policy Officer (Targeting Analyst) – Rome, Italy

Both assignments are offered for an initial duration of two years and provide extensive professional development, mentorship, international exposure, and hands-on experience within one of the most respected humanitarian organizations in the world.

About the World Food Programme

The World Food Programme is the leading humanitarian organization fighting global hunger. Operating in more than 120 countries and territories, WFP saves lives during emergencies and supports communities in rebuilding livelihoods after crises caused by conflict, natural disasters, economic shocks, and climate-related events.

As a global organization, WFP values:

  • Integrity
  • Collaboration
  • Commitment
  • Humanity
  • Inclusion

The organization is committed to diversity, equal opportunity, and creating an inclusive workplace where employees from various backgrounds can thrive and contribute meaningfully.

Why Consider a Career with WFP?

Working with the World Food Programme offers far more than a traditional job. Successful candidates will join a global network of professionals dedicated to improving lives around the world.

Key benefits include:

  • Working for a Nobel Peace Prize-winning organization
  • Attractive compensation package
  • Extensive professional training opportunities
  • Access to mentoring and coaching programmes
  • International career mobility opportunities
  • Exposure to global humanitarian operations
  • Opportunities to work alongside leading experts in humanitarian assistance
  • Development of technical and leadership competencies
  • Experience within the United Nations system
  • Meaningful work that directly impacts vulnerable populations worldwide

Opportunity 1: JPO Logistics Officer – Dakar, Senegal

Position Overview

The JPO Logistics Officer will be based at the Western and Central Africa Regional Office (WACARO) in Dakar, Senegal.

The role supports WFP’s regional logistics and supply chain operations across Western and Central Africa, a region characterized by complex humanitarian operations and growing emergency response needs.

The selected candidate will work under the supervision of the Regional Head of Logistics and contribute to strengthening logistics systems that enable humanitarian assistance to reach millions of beneficiaries.

Key Responsibilities

The Logistics Officer will:

  • Support logistics planning and operational processes
  • Contribute to logistics projects and regional supply chain initiatives
  • Gather and analyze logistics data from humanitarian actors
  • Identify operational bottlenecks and recommend improvements
  • Support procurement, shipping, port operations, and customs clearance processes
  • Coordinate transport and warehouse service contracts
  • Assist country offices during emergency responses
  • Develop logistics reports, situation updates, and operational overviews
  • Support staff and partner capacity-building activities
  • Participate in logistics preparedness assessments
  • Review and improve logistics procedures and standard operating procedures
  • Update logistics maps and operational documentation
  • Conduct field missions across the region

Expected Learning Outcomes

At the end of the assignment, the JPO is expected to gain:

  • Advanced knowledge of WFP logistics systems
  • Expertise in humanitarian supply chain management
  • Strong operational planning skills
  • Experience supporting emergency responses
  • Regional field experience across Western and Central Africa
  • Strategic understanding of humanitarian logistics operations

Preferred Experience

Candidates with experience in the following areas may be particularly competitive:

  • Transportation management
  • Customs clearance processes
  • Freight monitoring
  • Warehouse operations
  • Commodity management
  • Supply chain software and databases
  • International humanitarian operations

Opportunity 2: JPO Programme Policy Officer (Targeting Analyst) – Rome, Italy

Position Overview

The JPO Programme Policy Officer (Targeting Analyst) will be based at WFP Headquarters in Rome, Italy, within the Analysis, Performance and Planning Division.

The position focuses on improving targeting systems and methodologies that help WFP identify and prioritize populations most in need of assistance.

The officer will collaborate with headquarters teams, regional offices, country offices, and external stakeholders to strengthen evidence-based decision-making and improve humanitarian programme effectiveness.

Key Responsibilities

The Targeting Analyst will:

  • Draft policy documents and technical guidance materials
  • Support research and innovation initiatives related to targeting methodologies
  • Document lessons learned and operational best practices
  • Produce learning resources and training materials
  • Facilitate workshops and webinars
  • Monitor policy and strategic frameworks
  • Conduct operational reviews and field missions
  • Support implementation of corporate targeting guidance
  • Provide technical assistance to country offices
  • Contribute to the Global Assurance Project
  • Support benchmark development and performance tracking

Expected Learning Outcomes

Successful candidates will develop:

  • Strong expertise in targeting analysis
  • Advanced understanding of humanitarian programming
  • Knowledge of food security and nutrition programming
  • Experience in policy development and implementation
  • Skills in capacity building and technical guidance
  • Familiarity with WFP’s global operations and frameworks

Training and Professional Development

Both positions provide extensive learning opportunities, including:

  • On-the-job training
  • Mentorship from experienced supervisors
  • Access to WFP’s corporate learning platforms
  • Participation in workshops and professional courses
  • International missions and field assignments
  • Exposure to regional and global humanitarian operations
  • Cross-functional collaboration opportunities

These assignments are designed to prepare professionals for long-term careers within international organizations and the broader humanitarian sector.

Working Environment

WFP offers a highly inclusive and multicultural workplace where employees collaborate across regions, cultures, and disciplines.

The organization promotes:

  • Equal employment opportunities
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Professional growth
  • Staff wellbeing
  • Ethical leadership
  • Continuous learning

Applications are encouraged from qualified candidates regardless of race, colour, ethnic background, gender, religion, disability status, sexual orientation, or social background.

Application Requirements

Applicants should prepare:

  • Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Cover Letter
  • Complete Workday profile
  • Academic qualifications
  • Language proficiency information
  • Employment history details

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for subsequent stages of the recruitment process.

Important Application Deadlines

JPO Programme Policy Officer (Targeting Analyst) – Rome, Italy

Deadline: 8 June 2026 (23:59 CET)

JPO Logistics Officer – Dakar, Senegal

Deadline: 10 June 2026 (23:59 GMT)

CLICK TO APPLY

These Junior Professional Officer opportunities represent an exceptional pathway into the United Nations system and provide a unique chance to contribute to life-saving humanitarian operations while building a globally recognized international career.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here.

The post World Food Programme Junior Professional Officer Opportunities 2026 for Canadian Nationals: Fully Funded International Humanitarian Careers in Senegal and Italy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Africa Conference and Expo 2026: Africa’s Premier Platform for Sustainable Business, Innovation, and Leadership
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Join Africa’s Leading Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Event in Johannesburg, South Africa The Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) Africa Conference and Expo 2026 is set to take place from 30 September to 1 October 2026 at the prestigious Sandton Convention Centre, Johannesburg, South Africa. Recognized as Africa’s largest and most influential […]

The post Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Africa Conference and Expo 2026: Africa’s Premier Platform for Sustainable Business, Innovation, and Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Join Africa’s Leading Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Event in Johannesburg, South Africa

The Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) Africa Conference and Expo 2026 is set to take place from 30 September to 1 October 2026 at the prestigious Sandton Convention Centre, Johannesburg, South Africa. Recognized as Africa’s largest and most influential executive platform focused on sustainability and ESG, the conference brings together business leaders, policymakers, investors, innovators, academics, and sustainability practitioners to explore the future of sustainable business across the continent.

As sustainability continues to evolve from a compliance-driven requirement into a core business strategy, organizations are increasingly being challenged to demonstrate how sustainability contributes to profitability, resilience, capital access, and long-term value creation. The Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo 2026 aims to provide practical answers to these challenges while fostering collaboration and innovation among stakeholders shaping Africa’s sustainable future.

Why the Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo 2026 Matters

The sustainability landscape has undergone a significant transformation in recent years. Organizations are no longer judged solely on their sustainability reports or environmental commitments. Instead, investors, customers, regulators, and stakeholders expect businesses to demonstrate measurable outcomes and real economic value from their sustainability initiatives.

The conference addresses a critical question facing organizations today:

Does sustainability make business sense?

Through expert-led discussions, case studies, masterclasses, exhibitions, and networking opportunities, participants will gain valuable insights into how sustainability can drive business growth, operational resilience, and long-term competitiveness.

Event Details

Conference Dates

  • 30 September – 1 October 2026

Venue

  • Sandton Convention Centre
  • Sandton, Johannesburg, South Africa

Location Address

  • 161 Maude Street
  • Sandown, Sandton, 2196
  • Johannesburg, South Africa

What Makes This Conference Unique?

The Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo has established itself as the continent’s most trusted sustainability gathering. It offers a unique platform where sustainability is discussed not only as a social responsibility issue but as a strategic business imperative.

Key Highlights Include:

1. Africa’s Premier ESG Platform

The conference is widely regarded as Africa’s largest and longest-running sustainability and ESG event. It serves as a meeting point for influential leaders driving sustainability initiatives across various industries.

2. Access to Influential Leaders

Participants gain access to more than 100 sustainability leaders, executives, policymakers, and experts who are actively shaping the future of responsible business and sustainable development across Africa.

3. Year-Round Sustainability Community

The conference extends beyond the two-day event through ongoing engagement opportunities, fostering continuous collaboration, networking, and learning throughout the year.

4. Sustainability Solutions Exhibition

The expo features dedicated exhibition spaces showcasing innovative sustainability products, services, technologies, and business solutions designed to address real-world challenges.

5. Recognition of Excellence

The event hosts prestigious award programs that celebrate sustainability leadership, innovation, and impactful contributions to ESG advancement across Africa.

These include:

  • Beyond Awards
  • YES Awards
  • Student Innovation Awards

6. Practical Masterclasses

Attendees can participate in hands-on learning experiences designed to help organizations move from sustainability strategy to practical implementation.

7. Executive-Level Networking

The CEO Breakfast provides a unique opportunity for C-suite executives and senior decision-makers to engage in strategic discussions on the future of sustainability.

8. Real Impact Stories

Industry leaders share practical experiences, measurable outcomes, lessons learned, and proven approaches to implementing sustainability initiatives successfully.

9. Africa-Centric Solutions

Unlike many global sustainability events, this conference is specifically designed to address Africa’s unique opportunities, challenges, and development priorities while maintaining global relevance.

Key Benefits of Attending

Participants can expect numerous benefits, including:

Professional Development

  • Learn from leading sustainability experts.
  • Gain practical knowledge on ESG implementation.
  • Stay informed about emerging sustainability trends.

Networking Opportunities

  • Connect with industry leaders.
  • Build relationships with investors and policymakers.
  • Collaborate with sustainability practitioners across sectors.

Business Growth

  • Discover sustainability solutions that improve business performance.
  • Explore new partnership opportunities.
  • Identify strategies for accessing sustainable finance.

Innovation Exposure

  • Learn about cutting-edge sustainability technologies.
  • Explore innovative business models.
  • Engage with emerging sustainability startups and innovators.

Strategic Insights

  • Understand evolving ESG regulations and expectations.
  • Learn how sustainability can enhance organizational resilience.
  • Discover strategies for long-term value creation.

Additional Learning Opportunities Throughout the Year

Beyond the flagship conference, the Sustainability and ESG Africa platform hosts several events designed to encourage continuous engagement and knowledge sharing.

Community of Practice Session: Climate Adaptation and Nature-Based Solutions

Date: 26 May 2026
Time: 16:00 – 18:00
Format: Online (Zoom)

This session focuses on:

  • Climate adaptation strategies.
  • Nature-based solutions.
  • Building organizational resilience.
  • Addressing climate-related risks.
  • Long-term sustainability planning.

Webinar: The Future of Voluntary Carbon Markets in Africa – From Potential to Value

Date: 11 June 2026
Time: 15:00 – 16:30
Format: Online (Zoom)

Key discussion areas include:

  • Opportunities within Africa’s voluntary carbon market.
  • Policy and regulatory considerations.
  • Responsible participation frameworks.
  • Sustainable development impacts.
  • Strategies for capturing long-term value.

What Previous Attendees Say

Past participants consistently describe the Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference as a transformative experience that goes beyond traditional conference formats.

Common themes highlighted by attendees include:

  • Meaningful networking opportunities.
  • High-quality discussions and thought leadership.
  • Practical and actionable sustainability insights.
  • Exposure to innovative solutions.
  • Strong focus on African sustainability realities.
  • Opportunities to engage with future innovators.
  • Cross-sector collaboration and knowledge sharing.

Industry leaders from organizations such as consulting firms, financial institutions, multinational corporations, technology companies, mining organizations, and sustainability-focused enterprises have praised the event for its relevance, impact, and practical value.

Who Should Attend?

The conference is ideal for:

  • Corporate executives and CEOs.
  • Sustainability and ESG managers.
  • Investors and financial professionals.
  • Policymakers and regulators.
  • Entrepreneurs and business owners.
  • Climate and environmental specialists.
  • Supply chain professionals.
  • Researchers and academics.
  • Students and emerging innovators.
  • Development practitioners.
  • Non-governmental organizations.
  • Consultants and advisors.

Sustainability and ESG: The Future of Business in Africa

As sustainability becomes increasingly central to business success, organizations must adapt to changing stakeholder expectations, investor demands, and market realities. The Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo 2026 offers an invaluable platform for learning, collaboration, and action.

By bringing together diverse stakeholders from across Africa and beyond, the conference helps drive meaningful conversations, practical solutions, and measurable impact in the sustainability space.

Whether you are a seasoned sustainability professional, a business executive seeking strategic insights, an investor exploring sustainable opportunities, or a student passionate about shaping a better future, this conference provides the knowledge, connections, and inspiration needed to advance sustainability and ESG goals.

Deadline

There is currently no application deadline specified for attendance, sponsorship, exhibition opportunities, or participation. Interested individuals and organizations are encouraged to register or secure participation opportunities as early as possible.

Application and Further Information

Official Website: https://esgafricaconference.com/

For inquiries:

  • Email: hannelie@esgafricaconference.com

Stay connected with the Sustainability and ESG Africa community and be part of the movement shaping the future of sustainable business across Africa.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Conference Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Africa Conference and Expo 2026: Africa’s Premier Platform for Sustainable Business, Innovation, and Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 Applications Now Open | Join NASA’s Next Generation of Earth Observation Innovators
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has officially opened applications for the NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 cohort, an exciting professional development and applied research opportunity designed to prepare the next generation of Earth observation and geospatial technology leaders. The EarthRISE Developers Academy offers participants the opportunity to work on real-world interdisciplinary projects […]

The post NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 Applications Now Open | Join NASA’s Next Generation of Earth Observation Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has officially opened applications for the NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 cohort, an exciting professional development and applied research opportunity designed to prepare the next generation of Earth observation and geospatial technology leaders.

The EarthRISE Developers Academy offers participants the opportunity to work on real-world interdisciplinary projects using NASA Earth observation data to solve pressing global challenges. Through collaborative learning, applied research, networking, and mentorship, the Academy equips participants with practical experience in Earth science applications, GIS, remote sensing, and decision-support technologies.

The Fall 2026 term will run from September 14 to November 20, 2026, and applications are currently open until June 26, 2026.

About NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy

NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy is a workforce development initiative led by NASA Langley Research Center. Building on more than 25 years of workforce development expertise, the program immerses participants in short-term applied science projects that use NASA data and technologies to address real-world environmental and societal issues.

The Academy focuses on developing innovative applied science solutions that help organizations make more effective and efficient decisions. Participants collaborate with state, local, tribal, territorial, and private-sector partners while gaining hands-on experience in using Earth observation technologies for practical applications.

The program also helps bridge Earth science with the future of space exploration by preparing participants with interdisciplinary technical and professional skills relevant to the rapidly growing geospatial and Earth observation workforce.

Key Program Details

Application Deadline

June 26, 2026

Program Dates

September 14 – November 20, 2026

Program Format

  • Virtual and agency-wide opportunities available
  • Collaborative interdisciplinary team projects
  • Short-term applied research experience

NASA Earth Action Thematic Areas

Projects within the EarthRISE Developers Academy typically focus on one or more of NASA’s Earth Action thematic priorities, including:

  1. Agriculture
  2. Disasters
  3. Ecological Conservation
  4. Energy & Infrastructure
  5. Health & Air Quality
  6. Water Resources
  7. Wildland Fires

These thematic areas are designed to address major environmental and societal challenges through data-driven innovation and Earth observation technologies.

Who Should Apply?

NASA encourages applications from individuals with interdisciplinary backgrounds and varying levels of professional experience. The program welcomes:

  • Currently enrolled students
  • Recent graduates
  • Early-career professionals
  • Transitioning career professionals

Applicants with experience in the following areas are particularly encouraged to apply:

  • Earth Science
  • Environmental Science
  • Planetary Science
  • Geographic Information Systems (GIS)
  • Remote Sensing
  • Computer Science
  • Data Analytics
  • Earth Observation Applications

Successful participants are typically:

  • Open-minded
  • Collaborative
  • Adaptable
  • Eager to learn
  • Interested in interdisciplinary problem-solving

What Participants Will Do

Participants in the EarthRISE Developers Academy work in interdisciplinary teams of approximately four to five members in a fast-paced collaborative environment.

Each team works on an applied research project designed to help partner organizations use NASA Earth observation data to improve workflows, planning, and management decisions.

Program activities typically include:

1. Literature Review and Research

Participants conduct reviews on:

  • Relevant scientific methodologies
  • NASA remote sensing datasets
  • Existing applications and research approaches
  • Scope and background of project topics

2. GIS and Remote Sensing Applications

Participants use:

  • GIS technologies
  • Satellite imagery
  • NASA Earth observation datasets
  • Remote sensing tools

to create practical application solutions for partner organizations.

3. Team Collaboration

Participants engage in:

  • Interdisciplinary teamwork
  • Applied problem-solving
  • Communication and project coordination
  • Collaborative research development

4. Final Deliverables

At the end of the program, teams complete:

  • A final presentation for multiple audiences
  • A technical report
  • Project end products and application solutions

Mentorship and Guidance

Each project team receives support from:

  • Project coordinators
  • Science advisors
  • Professional mentors
  • NASA Langley Research Center’s EarthRISE Office

This mentorship structure allows participants to gain exposure to real-world scientific workflows and professional collaboration environments.

Benefits of Joining the NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy

The Academy offers extensive personal, technical, and professional development opportunities.

Participants can expect to strengthen skills in:

Technical Skills

  • GIS analysis
  • Remote sensing
  • Earth observation applications
  • Data-driven research
  • Scientific communication

Professional Skills

  • Presentation and public speaking
  • Team collaboration
  • Networking
  • Technical writing
  • Project development

Career Development

Participants also gain:

  • Exposure to NASA projects and scientists
  • Experience working with partner organizations
  • Interdisciplinary research experience
  • Insight into the geospatial workforce
  • Professional networking opportunities

The Academy also includes personality typing exercises, team-building experiences, and interactions with experts working in Earth science and applied technology sectors.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As global challenges related to climate, disasters, water resources, air quality, and ecological conservation continue to grow, there is increasing demand for professionals who can apply Earth observation data to real-world decision-making.

NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy provides participants with a rare opportunity to:

  • Work directly with NASA-related projects
  • Gain applied research experience
  • Build geospatial and Earth observation expertise
  • Develop interdisciplinary collaboration skills
  • Prepare for careers in the evolving Earth science and space technology sectors

The program is especially valuable for individuals interested in careers involving:

  • Climate and environmental science
  • Geospatial technology
  • Space applications
  • Sustainability and resilience
  • Remote sensing and satellite data analysis

How to Apply

Interested applicants should carefully follow these steps:

Step 1: Read the Application Guide

Review the complete application guide and eligibility requirements carefully.

Step 2: Review Logistics and Project Information

Explore available projects and program logistics. NASA notes that project information may continue to be updated during the application period.

Step 3: Access the Application Portal

Use the official EarthRISE Developers Academy Application Portal to begin and submit your application.

Step 4: Prepare Supporting Materials

Ensure all required application materials are completed and submitted before the deadline.

Official NASA Resources

Applicants are encouraged to consult the official NASA EarthRISE pages for:

  • Application guidelines
  • Eligibility details
  • Project descriptions
  • Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
  • Program logistics

For additional inquiries, applicants may contact the NASA Langley EarthRISE Office directly through the official contact provided on the NASA website.

Final Thoughts

The NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for students, researchers, and early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience working with NASA Earth observation data and applied science solutions.

Through interdisciplinary collaboration, mentorship, and real-world research projects, participants can build valuable technical and professional skills while contributing to solutions for major global environmental challenges.

For anyone interested in Earth science, GIS, remote sensing, environmental applications, or space-related innovation, this program offers a powerful pathway into the future of the geospatial and Earth observation workforce.

Application Deadline: June 26, 2026
Program Dates: September 14 – November 20, 2026

Learn More and Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE to learn more about the NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 Applications Now Open | Join NASA’s Next Generation of Earth Observation Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Agentic AI Foundation Ambassador Program 2026: Apply to Join the First Global Cohort of Open Source AI Advocates
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF), a Linux Foundation initiative focused on advancing open-source agentic AI technologies, has officially launched its highly anticipated AAIF Ambassador Program 2026. Applications are now open for the inaugural cohort, with submissions being accepted until June 12, 2026. This newly launched global ambassador initiative is designed for developers, educators, creators, and […]

The post Agentic AI Foundation Ambassador Program 2026: Apply to Join the First Global Cohort of Open Source AI Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF), a Linux Foundation initiative focused on advancing open-source agentic AI technologies, has officially launched its highly anticipated AAIF Ambassador Program 2026. Applications are now open for the inaugural cohort, with submissions being accepted until June 12, 2026.

This newly launched global ambassador initiative is designed for developers, educators, creators, and AI practitioners who are passionate about open-source AI and committed to helping others learn how to build with cutting-edge agentic AI technologies.

The program aims to create a strong ecosystem of advocates who will educate the developer community, increase awareness of AAIF-hosted projects, and encourage broader participation in open-source AI development.

What is the AAIF Ambassador Program?

The AAIF Ambassador Program is an elite community initiative created by the Agentic AI Foundation to support project-based advocacy and developer education around agentic AI technologies.

The first cohort will include only 10 ambassadors, making this a highly selective and prestigious opportunity for individuals actively involved in the AI and open-source ecosystem.

Selected ambassadors will represent and promote AAIF projects through educational content, technical workshops, talks, tutorials, livestreams, courses, and community engagement activities.

The program specifically focuses on supporting awareness, practical adoption, and contribution pathways for major AAIF-hosted projects, including:

  • MCP
  • Goose
  • AGENTS.md

These projects are helping shape the future of agentic AI development and collaborative open-source AI systems.

Application Deadline

Applications for the first AAIF Ambassador Program cohort are open until:

June 12, 2026

AAIF also confirmed that future cohorts will be selected in January 2027, while applications remain open on a rolling basis throughout the year.

Goals of the AAIF Ambassador Program

The foundation outlined three major goals for the initiative:

1. Increase Awareness

AAIF wants ambassadors to help developers understand:

  • What agentic AI is
  • How AAIF projects work
  • Real-world use cases of agentic AI technologies
  • The problems these tools solve for developers and organizations

This will happen through public speaking, content creation, social media engagement, and community discussions.

2. Drive Activation

The program emphasizes hands-on learning and practical developer onboarding.

Ambassadors are encouraged to create resources such as:

  • Beginner tutorials
  • Demo projects
  • Technical walkthroughs
  • Livestream coding sessions
  • Quick-start guides
  • Educational videos

The objective is to help developers experience the value of AAIF projects quickly.

3. Encourage Contribution

AAIF also wants ambassadors to help developers become contributors to the open-source ecosystem.

This includes:

  • Explaining contribution pathways
  • Hosting workshops
  • Creating contributor guides
  • Collaborating with maintainers
  • Encouraging participation in the community

The foundation aims to build a sustainable contributor pipeline for its growing ecosystem.

Ambassador Responsibilities

Every selected ambassador is expected to make:

One meaningful public contribution every month

Each contribution must directly relate to an AAIF project and help developers better understand, use, or contribute to the technology.

Accepted Contribution Types

AAIF accepts multiple forms of technical and educational contributions, including:

Educational Tutorials

Create step-by-step guides showing developers how to get started with AAIF projects.

Conference Talks and Meetups

Speak at events about how AAIF technologies fit into the future of agentic AI development.

Video Content

Publish short videos demonstrating project features, workflows, or use cases.

Technical Blog Posts

Write detailed articles explaining developer challenges and how AAIF projects solve them.

Livestreams

Host coding sessions, walkthroughs, or interviews with project maintainers.

Online Courses

Build educational courses that teach developers how to build using AAIF technologies.

Benefits of Becoming an AAIF Ambassador

Selected ambassadors will gain access to numerous professional, networking, and visibility benefits.

Public Recognition

Ambassadors will receive:

  • Feature placement on the official AAIF Ambassador page
  • Promotion through AAIF social channels
  • Newsletter amplification
  • Community-wide recognition

Exclusive Insider Access

Participants will gain:

  • Access to a private Ambassador Discord community
  • Regular briefings with AAIF leadership
  • Direct interaction with project maintainers
  • Early visibility into project roadmaps and updates

Ambassador Swag and Credentials

Selected participants will receive:

  • Exclusive ambassador merchandise
  • Special event credentials
  • Official recognition materials

VIP Conference Experience

AAIF ambassadors may receive:

  • Discounted conference tickets
  • VIP seating access
  • Lounge access during events
  • Ambassador activities with AAIF leadership
  • Recognition during keynote sessions

Contribution Recognition System

The program also includes:

  • Points and badge systems
  • Status titles
  • Project-specific recognition badges
  • Long-term community visibility

Community Amplification

AAIF will actively help ambassadors grow their visibility by:

  • Sharing their content across official social platforms
  • Featuring their work in newsletters
  • Introducing them to project maintainers and ecosystem leaders

Who Should Apply?

The Agentic AI Foundation is looking for individuals who combine technical knowledge with community engagement and educational impact.

Strong candidates may include:

  • Open-source AI contributors
  • AI engineers and developers
  • Developer advocates
  • Technical writers
  • YouTubers and livestream creators
  • Community organizers
  • Workshop facilitators
  • AI educators
  • Technical bloggers
  • Conference speakers

Applicants do not need to meet every criterion, but should demonstrate a genuine interest in:

  • Open-source AI
  • Agentic AI systems
  • Developer education
  • Community growth

Why This Opportunity Matters

The AAIF Ambassador Program arrives at a time when interest in agentic AI systems is rapidly growing across the global technology ecosystem.

As organizations increasingly adopt autonomous and collaborative AI agents, open-source infrastructure projects like MCP, Goose, and AGENTS.md are becoming increasingly important.

This ambassador program offers selected individuals the opportunity to:

  • Build global visibility
  • Establish authority in AI and open-source communities
  • Network with AI leaders and maintainers
  • Gain early access to emerging technologies
  • Contribute to the future of open-source AI infrastructure

For developers and creators interested in becoming recognized voices in the AI ecosystem, this program represents a significant professional opportunity.

About the Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF)

The Agentic AI Foundation is a Linux Foundation initiative focused on advancing open-source technologies and ecosystems for agentic AI systems.

The foundation supports collaborative development, education, and adoption of open-source AI projects that enable intelligent autonomous workflows, multi-agent systems, and developer tooling for the next generation of AI applications.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official AAIF Ambassador Program page.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, with the first cohort intake taking place in June 2026.

Prospective ambassadors are encouraged to showcase:

  • Technical expertise
  • Educational content
  • Community involvement
  • Open-source contributions
  • Experience with developer advocacy

Final Thoughts

The launch of the AAIF Ambassador Program marks a major step forward for the global open-source agentic AI community.

By empowering educators, developers, and creators to share knowledge and build practical learning pathways, the initiative aims to accelerate the adoption and understanding of agentic AI technologies worldwide.

With only 10 spots available in the inaugural cohort, competition is expected to be strong. Applicants interested in becoming early advocates of open-source agentic AI are encouraged to apply before the June 12, 2026 deadline.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Agentic AI Foundation Ambassador Program 2026.

Explore more fully funded PhD opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Agentic AI Foundation Ambassador Program 2026: Apply to Join the First Global Cohort of Open Source AI Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), with financial support from the Government of Germany, has launched applications for the Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants (DBG) 2026. This funding opportunity is designed to empower local organizations working to restore degraded landscapes, strengthen climate resilience, improve biodiversity, and create sustainable livelihood opportunities […]

The post FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), with financial support from the Government of Germany, has launched applications for the Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants (DBG) 2026. This funding opportunity is designed to empower local organizations working to restore degraded landscapes, strengthen climate resilience, improve biodiversity, and create sustainable livelihood opportunities across selected African countries.

Through this grant scheme, eligible organizations can access funding ranging from USD 5,000 to USD 50,000 to implement practical restoration initiatives that directly benefit communities and ecosystems.

The initiative forms part of the broader AFR100 (African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative), which seeks to restore millions of hectares of degraded land across Africa while supporting economic development and environmental sustainability.

About the Supporting AFR100 Programme

The Supporting AFR100 Programme is being implemented by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and funded by the Government of Germany.

The programme aims to strengthen locally led forest and landscape restoration efforts in selected African landscapes by supporting community-based organizations and local actors that are actively involved in restoring degraded lands and promoting sustainable natural resource management.

The programme is currently being implemented in:

  • Democratic Republic of the Congo
  • Kenya
  • Madagascar
  • Malawi
  • Togo
  • United Republic of Tanzania

By supporting grassroots restoration efforts, the programme seeks to build stronger ecosystems, increase climate resilience, improve food security, and generate economic opportunities for local communities.

Funding Available

Under the Direct Beneficiary Grants mechanism, eligible organizations can apply for grants ranging from:

  • Minimum Grant: USD 5,000
  • Maximum Grant: USD 50,000

Funding will be provided in local currency equivalent amounts.

The grants are intended to support practical and measurable restoration interventions that generate positive environmental and socioeconomic outcomes.

Objectives of the Direct Beneficiary Grants

The Direct Beneficiary Grants scheme aims to support organizations that are:

  • Restoring degraded lands
  • Enhancing community resilience
  • Improving livelihoods
  • Promoting sustainable land management
  • Strengthening restoration-based value chains
  • Supporting biodiversity conservation
  • Increasing ecosystem productivity
  • Encouraging climate adaptation and mitigation efforts

The programme recognizes that local communities play a central role in restoring landscapes and protecting natural resources. As such, the funding is designed to strengthen local ownership and leadership in restoration initiatives.

Eligible Activities

Organizations may seek funding for a broad range of restoration-related interventions, including but not limited to:

1. Tree Planting Initiatives

Projects that promote afforestation and reforestation activities to restore degraded lands and improve ecosystem health.

2. Agroforestry Projects

Activities integrating trees into agricultural systems to improve productivity, biodiversity, and climate resilience.

3. Soil and Water Conservation

Initiatives focused on:

  • Erosion control
  • Watershed management
  • Water harvesting
  • Sustainable soil management practices

4. Community Nurseries

Establishment and strengthening of nurseries for producing quality seedlings and supporting restoration efforts.

5. Biodiversity Conservation

Projects aimed at:

  • Protecting native species
  • Restoring habitats
  • Conserving ecosystems
  • Enhancing ecological connectivity

6. Restoration-Based Livelihood Activities

Income-generating activities linked to restoration efforts that support local economic development while protecting natural resources.

7. Sustainable Value Chain Development

Projects that strengthen restoration-related value chains and create economic opportunities for local communities.

Why This Grant Matters

Across Africa, millions of hectares of land have been degraded due to unsustainable land use, deforestation, climate change, and biodiversity loss. Restoration efforts are increasingly recognized as a critical strategy for addressing these challenges.

The AFR100 Programme supports locally driven solutions that can:

  • Improve food security
  • Increase climate resilience
  • Restore ecosystem services
  • Create green jobs
  • Support sustainable agriculture
  • Strengthen rural livelihoods
  • Protect biodiversity
  • Enhance water resources
  • Reduce land degradation

By investing directly in local organizations, the programme ensures that restoration solutions are community-led and adapted to local realities.

Who Should Consider Applying?

This opportunity is particularly suitable for:

  • Community-based organizations
  • Environmental NGOs
  • Conservation organizations
  • Agroforestry groups
  • Farmer associations
  • Rural development organizations
  • Natural resource management groups
  • Restoration-focused cooperatives
  • Biodiversity conservation organizations

Organizations with proven experience implementing restoration activities within the designated programme landscapes are especially encouraged to apply.

Application Process

Before beginning the application process, applicants should carefully review the official Applicant Guidelines to ensure compliance with all eligibility requirements.

Organizations should ensure they:

  1. Confirm eligibility.
  2. Review the Applicant Guidelines thoroughly.
  3. Prepare all required documentation.
  4. Develop a clear and realistic project proposal.
  5. Demonstrate how proposed activities contribute to restoration and community resilience.
  6. Submit their application before the deadline.

Key Benefits of the Programme

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Grant funding between USD 5,000 and USD 50,000
  • Support for locally led restoration initiatives
  • Opportunity to strengthen community resilience
  • Capacity to improve restoration-based livelihoods
  • Contribution to national and continental restoration goals
  • Increased environmental and social impact
  • Enhanced ecosystem restoration outcomes
  • Support from a globally recognized United Nations agency

Important Dates

Application Opening Date

22 May 2026

Application Deadline

19 June 2026 at 11:59 PM (GMT)

Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

Apply Here 

Need More Information?

Organizations seeking clarification regarding the grant opportunity can contact the programme team during the application period through:

Email: afr-100-programme@fao.org

Prospective applicants should consult the official Applicant Guidelines and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements before submitting their proposals.

The Supporting AFR100 Programme represents an important opportunity for local organizations across Africa to access funding that can help restore degraded landscapes, strengthen livelihoods, and contribute to a more resilient and sustainable future.

For more information on this grant opportunity click here

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, visit here  

The post FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) Presidential Fellow 2026–2027: Earn Up to $200,000 While Working Directly with the President of One of the World’s Leading Science Organizations
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) has opened applications for its highly competitive Presidential Fellow Program, an exceptional opportunity designed for outstanding early-career professionals who want to work at the highest levels of leadership, strategy, innovation, and scientific philanthropy. Based at HHMI Headquarters in Chevy Chase, Maryland, USA, the fellowship places a small cohort of […]

The post Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) Presidential Fellow 2026–2027: Earn Up to $200,000 While Working Directly with the President of One of the World’s Leading Science Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) has opened applications for its highly competitive Presidential Fellow Program, an exceptional opportunity designed for outstanding early-career professionals who want to work at the highest levels of leadership, strategy, innovation, and scientific philanthropy.

Based at HHMI Headquarters in Chevy Chase, Maryland, USA, the fellowship places a small cohort of only 2–3 fellows per year directly within the Office of the President. Unlike traditional fellowships that focus primarily on observation or rotations, HHMI Presidential Fellows are entrusted with leading strategic projects, engaging with senior executives, and helping shape the future direction of one of the world’s most influential scientific organizations.

With a salary ranging from $150,000 to $200,000 annually, this fellowship offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious young professionals interested in science, technology, artificial intelligence, policy, philanthropy, education, communications, and organizational leadership.

About Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI)

Howard Hughes Medical Institute is one of the world’s largest private funders of biomedical research.

The organization supports:

  • Scientists at leading universities
  • Research institutes
  • Hospitals
  • Science education initiatives
  • Film and media projects
  • Artificial intelligence-enabled research programs

HHMI is widely recognized for making long-term investments in talented individuals whose work has the potential to transform science and society.

Its headquarters, located in Maryland near Washington, D.C., houses experts from numerous disciplines, including:

  • Biomedical sciences
  • Investments
  • Communications
  • Human resources
  • Technology
  • Digital production
  • Operations
  • Strategic planning

Through these efforts, HHMI contributes to groundbreaking discoveries and educational initiatives that impact people around the world.
